walkthrough.title: Choose an Airport
walkthrough.text: Let's get set up for landing! First, pick an airport card. If this is your first game, use Montreal, which is the beginner airport. Each airport has different challenges and modules, so you will unlock harder destinations as you play.

walkthrough.title: Place the Cockpit Board
walkthrough.text: Set the cockpit board between both players. This is your shared control panel where you will place dice throughout the game. The left side belongs to the Captain, and the right side belongs to the Co-pilot. Each slot on the board represents a different system on the airplane.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Approach Track
walkthrough.text: Place the approach board next to the cockpit. This track shows the altitude levels your plane must descend through. Put the airplane standee at the very top of the track. Your goal is to guide it safely to the bottom for a smooth landing.

walkthrough.title: Place Traffic and Set Markers
walkthrough.text: Let's get the sky busy. Place airplane tokens on the approach track wherever the airport card indicates. These are other planes in your flight path that you must clear before descending to their altitude. Then set the axis marker to the center position and the speed marker to the starting speed.

walkthrough.title: Prepare Landing Gear and Flaps
walkthrough.text: Place the three landing gear tokens and two flap tokens in their undeployed positions on the cockpit board. You will need to deploy all of these before landing. Missing even one means a crash on the runway.

walkthrough.title: Assign Roles and Dice
walkthrough.text: One player sits on the left and becomes the Captain, taking four orange dice. The other player sits on the right as the Co-pilot, taking four blue dice. You keep these roles for the entire game. Place any coffee tokens indicated by the airport card into the supply.

walkthrough.title: Understand the Communication Rule
walkthrough.text: Here is the most important rule in Sky Team. You cannot tell your partner what numbers are on your dice. You can discuss general strategy and priorities, but you cannot reveal, hint at, or discuss specific dice values. This restriction is the heart of the game.

walkthrough.title: Ready for Takeoff
walkthrough.text: Both players roll their four dice secretly behind a screen or cupped hands. Once both are ready, reveal all dice simultaneously. The Captain places first, then you alternate placing one die at a time. After all eight dice are placed, resolve the round. Let's learn how that works.

summary.title: Choose an Airport
bullets: Pick an airport card — Montreal for beginners
Each airport adds unique challenges and modules

Airplane standee starts at the top (highest altitude)

bullets: Traffic planes on approach track per airport card
Axis marker → center; speed marker → starting position

bullets: 3 landing gear tokens + 2 flap tokens → undeployed positions
All must be deployed before landing

Roles are permanent for the game
Place coffee tokens per airport card

summary.title: Understand the Communication Rule
bullets: Cannot reveal, hint at, or discuss specific dice values
General strategy discussion is allowed
This restriction is the core challenge

summary.title: Ready for Takeoff
bullets: Both roll 4 dice secretly → reveal simultaneously
Captain places first → alternate 1 die at a time
All 8 placed → resolve round

walkthrough.title: Round Structure Overview
walkthrough.text: Let's walk through how each round works. Sky Team plays over several rounds, typically four to seven depending on the airport. Each round has the same rhythm: secretly roll, simultaneously reveal, alternate placing, then resolve. The plane descends after each round, so every decision gets more critical.

walkthrough.title: Rolling and Revealing Dice
walkthrough.text: At the start of each round, both players roll their four dice in secret. You can peek at your own dice but cannot share numbers with your partner. Once both players are ready, reveal all dice at the same time. Now you can see everything and must decide together where to place them, one at a time.

walkthrough.title: Alternating Dice Placement
walkthrough.text: Starting with the Captain, players take turns placing one die at a time into a cockpit slot. The Captain places an orange die, then the Co-pilot places a blue die, and so on. Each slot accepts only certain colors or has specific value requirements. You must place all eight dice before the round resolves.

walkthrough.title: The Axis Slot
walkthrough.text: Both players place one die on the axis. The Captain's die tilts the plane one direction, and the Co-pilot's die tilts it the other. The difference between the two values determines how much the plane tilts. If the axis marker goes too far in either direction, you crash instantly.

walkthrough.title: Engine and Speed Control
walkthrough.text: Place dice on the engine slots to control your descent speed. Higher values mean faster descent, which gets you to the ground quicker but gives you fewer rounds to prepare. Lower values mean a slower approach with more time. The speed determines how many spaces the plane moves down the approach track.

walkthrough.title: Landing Gear and Flaps
walkthrough.text: Deploy landing gear and flaps by placing dice that meet minimum value requirements. These must be deployed in order, first to third for gear and first to second for flaps. All three gear tokens and both flap tokens must be deployed before you reach the runway, or you crash.

walkthrough.title: Radio and Traffic Clearance
walkthrough.text: Traffic planes sit on the approach track at specific altitudes. If your plane descends to a space with uncleared traffic, you crash. Use the radio slot to clear traffic by placing a die whose value meets or exceeds the traffic plane's requirement. Plan ahead and clear traffic before you reach those altitudes.

walkthrough.title: Brakes and Concentration
walkthrough.text: The brake slot adds stopping power for your final approach. Place dice here to ensure you can slow down enough to land. The concentration slot lets you earn coffee tokens by placing a die. Coffee tokens are precious because they let you reroll one of your dice before placing it.

walkthrough.title: End of Round Resolution
walkthrough.text: After all eight dice are placed, resolve the round. Check the axis balance. Adjust the speed marker. Move the plane down the approach track by the current speed value. Check for traffic collisions. If any failure condition triggers, the game ends in a loss immediately.

walkthrough.title: Coffee Tokens
walkthrough.text: Spend a coffee token to reroll one of your own dice before placing it. This can rescue you from an impossible situation. Coffee tokens are earned by placing dice on the concentration slot, but the supply is limited. Use them wisely.

walkthrough.title: Landing Requirements
walkthrough.text: To land safely, you must meet all of these conditions when your plane reaches the runway. All three landing gear tokens deployed. Both flap tokens deployed. Speed at or below the landing threshold. Axis balanced at or near center. No traffic planes remaining in the landing zone. Miss even one and you crash.

walkthrough.title: Win and Loss Conditions
walkthrough.text: You win by landing the plane safely at the end of the approach track with all requirements met. You lose if the axis tilts too far, you hit traffic, your speed is too high to stop, gear or flaps are not deployed, or you overshoot the runway. Sky Team is a cooperative win or loss with no point scoring.

walkthrough.title: Welcome Aboard
walkthrough.text: We will walk through a practice approach to Montreal, the beginner airport. One of you is the Captain with orange dice, and the other is the Co-pilot with blue dice. Let's get our plane safely on the ground.

walkthrough.title: Round One: Roll and Reveal
walkthrough.text: Let's start our approach. Both players roll their four dice secretly. Remember, no peeking at each other's dice and no sharing numbers. Take a moment to look at your own dice and think about which slots you can fill. When ready, reveal all dice at the same time.

walkthrough.title: Prioritize the Axis
walkthrough.text: The Captain goes first. Look at the axis slot. This is your top priority every round. If the axis gets too far off center, the plane crashes immediately. Captain, place a die on the axis that you think will balance well with whatever the Co-pilot can offer. Then Co-pilot, place your axis die trying to match closely.

walkthrough.title: Axis Balance Check
walkthrough.text: Let's say the Captain placed a four and the Co-pilot placed a three. The difference is one, which shifts the axis one tick to the Captain's side. That is fine for now. If the difference had been four or five, we would be in serious trouble. Keep the axis balanced every round.

walkthrough.title: Place Remaining Dice
walkthrough.text: Continue alternating die placement. The Captain might put a die on the engine to control speed, while the Co-pilot places one on the radio to clear an upcoming traffic plane. Keep alternating until all eight dice are placed. Think about what your partner might need before committing your dice.

walkthrough.title: Round One Resolution
walkthrough.text: All dice placed. Check the axis: stable. Adjust speed. Move the plane down the approach track by the speed value. Check for traffic collisions. All clear! Great first round. Pick up your dice and get ready for round two.

walkthrough.title: The Co-pilot's Mistake: Ignoring Traffic
walkthrough.text: Round two begins. After revealing dice, the Co-pilot sees a low die and places it on concentration to grab a coffee token. Sounds nice, but there is a traffic plane two spaces ahead on the approach. Nobody places a die on the radio this round. After resolution, the plane descends right into the traffic. Crash.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Always Check the Approach
walkthrough.text: Before placing any dice, always look at the approach track first. Count how many spaces you will descend this round based on speed, and check if any traffic planes are in the way. Clearing traffic is not optional. It must happen before you reach that altitude. Coffee is nice, but survival comes first.

walkthrough.title: Restart: Round Two Take Two
walkthrough.text: Let's rewind and try again. This time, the Co-pilot uses that die on the radio instead, clearing the traffic plane just in time. The plane descends safely past where the traffic was. Crisis averted. Always scan the approach before placing dice.

walkthrough.title: The Captain's Mistake: Speed Too High
walkthrough.text: Round three. The Captain places a high value on the engine, pushing the speed up. The plane races down the approach track. But now we are running out of track with gear and flaps not fully deployed. We needed more rounds to finish deploying, but the high speed burned through our remaining approach distance.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Control Your Speed
walkthrough.text: Speed management is everything in Sky Team. Higher speed gets you down faster, but gives you fewer rounds to deploy gear, flaps, and clear traffic. For beginners, moderate or low speed is almost always better. Give yourself time to handle everything before worrying about getting down quickly.

walkthrough.title: The Captain's Mistake: Breaking Communication Rules
walkthrough.text: During round four, the Captain says, "I have a six, so I will handle the landing gear." That is against the rules. You cannot reveal specific die values to your partner. The correct way is to say something like, "I think I can handle landing gear this round." General intent, no specific numbers.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Communicate Intent, Not Numbers
walkthrough.text: The communication restriction is what makes Sky Team special. Say things like "I can cover the radio" or "Focus on the axis this round." Never say "I have a five" or "My highest die is a three." If you break this rule, you remove the core tension that makes the game exciting.

walkthrough.title: Final Approach and Landing
walkthrough.text: Let's jump ahead to the final round. All three landing gear tokens are deployed, both flaps are down, traffic is cleared, the axis is balanced, and speed is low enough to stop. The plane reaches the runway. All requirements met. Touchdown! You have landed safely.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations, Pilots
walkthrough.text: Congratulations, you have completed your first approach! You learned to prioritize the axis, clear traffic before it becomes a collision, manage your speed, and communicate without revealing numbers. Those are the four pillars of Sky Team.

walkthrough.title: Before Your Next Flight
walkthrough.text:
Before you tackle a harder airport, here are three things to help.

First, check out General Tips for communication strategies and priority management.

Second, reference the Appendix for a quick guide to all cockpit slots and modules.

And most importantly, ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Have fun!

walkthrough.title: Axis First, Always
walkthrough.text: The single most common cause of losing in Sky Team is axis imbalance. Every single round, your first priority should be the axis slot. Before thinking about traffic, gear, flaps, or anything else, make sure both players can contribute to a balanced axis. Even one unbalanced round can push you to the crash zone.

walkthrough.title: Communicate Priorities, Not Numbers
walkthrough.text: Let's talk about how to communicate effectively within the rules. Before revealing dice, discuss which slots matter most this round. Say things like "Traffic is urgent" or "We need gear deployed this round." After revealing, you can say "I will handle the radio" without mentioning values. Your partner will infer what they need to do.

walkthrough.title: Read Your Partner's Placements
walkthrough.text: Pay close attention to which slots your partner fills and what die values they use. If the Captain puts a low die on the engine, they are telling you they want slow speed. If the Co-pilot fills the radio, they are saying they have a high enough die to clear traffic. Every placement is a signal.

walkthrough.title: Manage Speed Carefully
walkthrough.text: Speed is the tempo of the game. Faster speed means fewer rounds to deploy gear, flaps, and clear traffic. Slower speed gives you more time but risks running out of approach track. For beginners, aim for moderate speed. As you get more experienced, you can push the speed higher when you are confident in your coordination.

walkthrough.title: Deploy Gear and Flaps Gradually
walkthrough.text: Do not wait until the last round to deploy everything. Spread your deployments across multiple rounds. Try to deploy one landing gear and one flap every other round. This way you are never in a panic trying to deploy three tokens in one round with limited dice.

walkthrough.title: Coffee Token Strategy
walkthrough.text: Coffee tokens are your safety net. Use them when you absolutely need a specific value and your current dice cannot deliver. Do not hoard them for a perfect moment that may never come. One well-timed reroll can prevent a crash. But also do not waste them on minor optimizations.

walkthrough.title: Scan the Approach Every Round
walkthrough.text: Before placing any dice, both players should look at the approach track together. Count how many spaces you will descend based on current speed. Check for traffic planes in that range. Identify which deployments are still needed. This five-second scan prevents the most common game-losing mistakes.

walkthrough.title: Trust Your Partner
walkthrough.text: Sky Team is a trust exercise. Sometimes you have to commit a die to a suboptimal slot because you trust your partner to cover a critical one. If you both try to handle everything solo, you will step on each other's toes and waste dice. Communicate intent, trust the other pilot, and fly as a team.

summary.title: Axis First, Always
bullets: Axis imbalance is the #1 cause of losing
Top priority every round before anything else
One bad round can push you to crash zone

Every placement is a signal — pay attention

Don't hoard — one timely reroll prevents crashes

walkthrough.title: Dice Value Communication Through Placement
walkthrough.text: Without saying numbers, you can communicate through your choices. If you place a one or two on the axis, your partner knows you have low dice. If you deploy a high-value gear token first, they know you had a high die to spare. Experienced teams develop an unspoken language through placement patterns.

walkthrough.title: Placement Order Strategy
walkthrough.text: The order you place dice matters as much as where you place them. Leading with the axis die signals your partner about your range. Placing on a contested slot early claims it before your partner has to decide. Experienced Captain players establish a consistent pattern so the Co-pilot can predict their moves.

walkthrough.title: Speed as a Strategic Lever
walkthrough.text: Advanced players manipulate speed intentionally. A fast descent in early rounds clears the safe part of the track quickly. Then slow down in later rounds when deployments and traffic get critical. This creates a buffer of extra rounds where you need them most. Think of speed as a resource, not a constant.

walkthrough.title: Coffee Token Efficiency
walkthrough.text: Advanced coffee strategy goes beyond emergency rerolls. Use coffee proactively to create value. Reroll a medium die into a high die for gear deployment. Reroll a die that is wasted on a low-impact slot into one that fills a critical need. The best time to spend coffee is early, when the benefit compounds over remaining rounds.

walkthrough.title: Traffic Prioritization Framework
walkthrough.text: Not all traffic planes need immediate attention. Calculate your descent path and identify which traffic is imminent and which is several rounds away. Clear imminent traffic first and save future traffic for later rounds when your dice might be better suited. Wasting a high die on distant traffic is inefficient.

walkthrough.title: Module Mastery
walkthrough.text: As you unlock harder airports, modules add complexity. Wind shifts the axis between rounds, requiring overcompensation. Ice blocks cockpit slots until cleared. Kerosene adds fuel management. Each module demands a specific adaptation. Study the module before starting and agree on a general plan with your partner.

walkthrough.title: Axis Manipulation for Flexibility
walkthrough.text: Advanced players intentionally let the axis drift slightly in safe directions to free up die values for other slots. If you know you can correct with a small difference next round, you can afford to use a suboptimal axis die now and put your best die on a critical slot like gear or radio.

walkthrough.title: Building Partner Synergy
walkthrough.text: The best Sky Team pairs develop a rhythm over multiple games. You learn which slots your partner prefers, how they signal priorities, and when they are likely to reroll. This synergy is the ultimate advanced strategy. Play multiple games with the same partner and develop your shared instincts.

summary.title: Dice Value Communication Through Placement
bullets: Low axis die = you have low dice overall
High gear deployment = you had a high die to spare
Placement patterns become unspoken language

summary.title: Placement Order Strategy
bullets: Lead with axis to signal your range
Claim contested slots early
Consistent patterns help partner predict

summary.title: Speed as a Strategic Lever
bullets: Fast early (safe track) → slow late (critical rounds)
Creates extra rounds where you need them
Treat speed as a resource, not a constant

summary.title: Coffee Token Efficiency
bullets: Use proactively, not just as emergency rerolls
Reroll medium → high for gear; low → useful for slots
Best spent early when benefit compounds

summary.title: Traffic Prioritization Framework
bullets: Clear imminent traffic first; save distant for later
Calculate descent path to identify urgency
Don't waste high dice on distant traffic

summary.title: Module Mastery

summary.title: Building Partner Synergy

Learn each other's slot preferences and signals
Shared instincts = ultimate advanced strategy

entries.definition: The player who sits on the left side and uses orange dice. The Captain always places the first die each round.
The player who sits on the right side and uses blue dice. The Co-pilot places after the Captain in alternating order.
The central board with all dice placement slots. Both players share this board, placing dice on their respective sides.
The board showing altitude levels the plane descends through. Traffic planes, the airplane standee, and landing zone are all on this track.
Both players place one die here each round. The difference between the two values determines how much the plane tilts. Extreme tilt causes a crash.
Controls the plane's descent speed. Higher die values increase speed, moving the plane further down the approach track each round.
Deploy landing gear by placing dice meeting minimum value requirements. Three gear tokens must be deployed in order before landing.
Deploy flaps by placing dice meeting minimum value requirements. Two flap tokens must be deployed in order before landing.
Clear traffic planes from the approach track. Die value must meet or exceed the traffic plane's clearance requirement.
Adds braking power for the final approach. Ensures the plane can slow down enough to land safely.
Place a die here to earn coffee tokens, which allow rerolling dice in future rounds.
Spend before placing a die to reroll it. Earned through the concentration slot. Limited supply that varies by airport.
Tracks the plane's left-right balance. Starts at center. Moves based on the difference between Captain and Co-pilot axis dice each round.
Tracks the plane's current descent speed. Determines how many spaces the plane moves down the approach track each round.
Airplane tokens placed on the approach track at specific altitudes. Must be cleared with the radio before your plane reaches that space, or you crash.
Players cannot reveal, hint at, or discuss specific dice values. General strategy, priorities, and intent may be discussed freely.
The win condition. All gear and flaps deployed, speed within threshold, axis balanced, approach clear of traffic, plane at runway.
Any failure condition that ends the game in a loss. Includes axis extremes, traffic collision, overshoot, or incomplete deployments at runway.
Defines the scenario: approach layout, traffic placement, starting conditions, modules used, and difficulty rating.
Optional mechanics added by harder airports. Include wind, ice, kerosene, and interns. Each adds a new layer of challenge.
